title: Rebuild a self-rescheduling worker without retry amplification
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/self_rescheduling_worker.ex` reschedules itself at the END of perform/1 — after `Digests.build_and_send(user)`. On a failed attempt (Digests.build_and_send raises or returns error), Oban retries perform/1, which re-executes both the business logic AND the reschedule call. This causes exponentially growing scheduled-job counts. Rewrite using the Sorentwo recipe: pattern-match two perform/1 clauses — the first clause matches `%Oban.Job{attempt: 1}` and schedules the NEXT occurrence FIRST (before calling Digests.build_and_send), the second clause matches all other attempts and ONLY does the business logic (no reschedule). Write to `lib/my_app/workers/digest_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: The retry-amplification bug is non-obvious and the fix requires two perform/1 clauses pattern-matched on attempt number. This is almost entirely absent from pre-2023 training data.
research_citation: Sorentwo 'Oban Recipes Part 3: Reliable Scheduling' — 'The first clause schedules the next iteration immediately, *before* attempting to deliver the email.'
